Title: AnDHN, a Dehydrin Protein From Ammopiptanthus nanus, Mitigates the Negative Effects of Drought Stress in Plants

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: AnDHN corresponds to transcript c195333_g1_i1 from A. nanus and is the only dehydrin identified so far from a drought-induced RNA-seq library of this species. Phylogenetic analysis places AnDHN in legume-enriched cluster VIII of angiosperm DHNs, closely related to Lup004542 (Lupinus angustifolius) and Ca_12999 (Cicer arietinum) and orthologous to Arabidopsis DHNs XERO1 (AT3G50980) and RAB18 (AT5G66400). The AnDHN promoter (~830 bp) contains multiple drought-, salt-, cold-, and hormone-responsive cis-elements and transcription-factor-binding sites, consistent with its inducible expression under these stimuli.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. AnDHN (c195333_g1_i1) was isolated from a drought-induced RNA-seq library of Ammopiptanthus nanus and encodes a dehydrin protein with conserved Y- and K-segment motifs.
2. AnDHN expression in A. nanus roots, stems, and leaves is strongly induced by drought (PEG 8000), and also by salt and cold stresses, consistent with the presence of multiple stress- and hormone-responsive cis-elements in its promoter.
3. AnDHN protein localizes to the plasma membrane in plant cells, as shown by AnDHN-GFP colocalization with a PM marker in Nicotiana benthamiana.
4. Overexpression of AnDHN in Arabidopsis enhances drought tolerance by increasing germination rates under mannitol stress, promoting deeper root system architecture, improving water retention and osmotic adjustment (higher RWC and proline, lower MDA), and enhancing ROS scavenging (higher POD, CAT, and GSH activities, lower ROS accumulation).
5. AnDHN overexpression simultaneously improves plant tolerance to salt and cold stresses, with higher germination and longer roots, improved water status and antioxidant capacity, and reduced ROS levels compared with WT under NaCl and low-temperature treatments.
6. In AnDHN-overexpressing Arabidopsis, ABA biosynthetic genes AtNCED3 and AtNCED5 are upregulated while catabolic genes AtCYP707A1 and AtCYP707A3 are downregulated, supporting a role for AnDHN in ABA-associated stress signaling and suggesting an AnDHN–ABA feedback loop.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Drought-induced RNA-seq of A. nanus seedlings used to identify upregulated transcript c195333_g1_i1 (AnDHN) as a candidate drought-responsive gene; expression changes shown as RPKM values.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of AnDHN in Arabidopsis markedly enhances tolerance to drought, salt, and cold stresses by improving seed germination and seedling survival under mannitol, NaCl, and low-temperature treatments, conferring deeper root system architecture under osmotic stress, and increasing water retention, osmotic adjustment, and ROS-scavenging capacity.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Membrane-localized dehydrin that enhances drought, salt, and cold tolerance by improving water retention (higher RWC), osmotic adjustment (proline accumulation), and ROS detoxification (increased POD, CAT, and GSH activities and reduced ROS accumulation). Overexpression of AnDHN correlates with upregulation of ABA biosynthetic genes (AtNCED3, AtNCED5) and downregulation of ABA catabolic genes (AtCYP707A1, AtCYP707A3), suggesting modulation of ABA levels and an AnDHN–ABA positive feedback loop.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in ABA-associated drought stress signaling and ROS-scavenging pathways: AnDHN is induced by ABA, and its overexpression in Arabidopsis is associated with increased expression of ABA biosynthetic genes and decreased expression of ABA catabolic genes, alongside enhanced antioxidant enzyme activities under drought.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: In Arabidopsis lines overexpressing the A. nanus AnDHN gene, expression of ABA biosynthetic genes AtNCED3 and AtNCED5 is significantly upregulated, while ABA catabolic genes AtCYP707A1 and AtCYP707A3 are downregulated, indicating that AnDHN acts upstream to positively influence ABA biosynthesis and negatively influence ABA catabolism.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Dehydrin (Group II LEA protein) harboring conserved Y-segment (DEYGNPV) and K-segment (KKGIMNKIKEKLPGY) motifs.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane; AnDHN-GFP fluorescence in N. benthamiana leaf epidermal cells overlapped with the plasma membrane marker PAD62.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In A. nanus, AnDHN is strongly induced by simulated drought (20% PEG 8000), with expression in roots significantly upregulated by 6 h and peaking at 24 h, and 2.8–3.1-fold induction in stems and leaves at 48 h relative to 0 h. AnDHN is also markedly induced in roots, stems, and leaves by salt (0.25 M NaCl) and cold (4°C) treatments, and responds to several hormones (ABA, IAA, NAA, MeJA, ETH, GA3) based on qRT–PCR and promoter element analysis.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Simulated drought with 20% PEG 8000 in A. nanus; NaCl (0.25 M in A. nanus, 150 mM in Arabidopsis assays); osmotic stress with mannitol (200–400 mM in germination, 300 mM in seedling assays); low temperature (4°C) for A. nanus treatments and Arabidopsis cold assays; hormone treatments including ABA, IAA, NAA, MeJA, ethylene, and GA3.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought, salt, cold) and ABA-related stress signaling in Ammopiptanthus nanus and transgenic Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance: drought tolerance, salt tolerance, cold tolerance; root system architecture under stress; ROS homeostasis and osmotic adjustment.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under 200–400 mM mannitol and 100–200 mM NaCl, AnDHN-OX lines exhibit significantly higher germination rates than WT; under 300 mM mannitol and 150 mM NaCl, AnDHN-OX seedlings have significantly longer roots than WT; under 300 mM mannitol, AnDHN-OX lines show significantly higher relative water content and proline levels and significantly lower MDA levels than WT, along with significantly higher POD, CAT, and GSH activities and reduced NBT/DAB staining under drought, salt, and cold conditions (all differences supported by Student’s t-test, p < 0.05 or p < 0.01).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: AnDHN-OX plants show increased proline accumulation and reduced MDA levels under drought and salt stress, lower H2O2 and O2− accumulation as revealed by DAB/NBT staining, and generally improved physiological status (higher POD, CAT, and GSH activities) under drought, salt, and cold treatments compared with WT.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: A. nanus seedlings (20-day-old, roots/stems/leaves); Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Col-0 wild type and homozygous AnDHN-overexpression T3 lines; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves co-infiltrated with 35S:AnDHN-GFP and plasma membrane marker PAD62.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Drought-induced RNA-seq screening; 5′/3′ RACE and cloning of AnDHN; qRT–PCR for expression profiling under PEG, NaCl, cold, and hormone treatments; promoter isolation by genome walking and in silico cis-element and TFBS prediction; subcellular localization of 35S:AnDHN-GFP in N. benthamiana using confocal microscopy; generation of stable Arabidopsis 35S:AnDHN transgenic lines via floral dip and hygromycin selection; abiotic stress assays (mannitol, NaCl, 4°C) measuring seed germination, root length, ion leakage, relative water content, proline and MDA contents, antioxidant enzyme activities (POD, CAT, SOD, GSH), and ROS accumulation by DAB/NBT staining; phylogenetic and motif analyses of DHN proteins.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression of AnDHN in Arabidopsis thaliana (35S:AnDHN) followed by germination and root growth assays under mannitol, NaCl, and 4°C treatments, and physiological/biochemical measurements (ion leakage, RWC, proline, MDA, antioxidant enzyme activities, ROS staining) to assess stress tolerance; subcellular localization of AnDHN-GFP to the plasma membrane.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a significantly upregulated drought-responsive transcript (c195333_g1_i1) from a drought-induced RNA-seq library of A. nanus seedlings, then full-length cDNA obtained by 5′ and 3′ RACE using gene-specific primers and assembled to yield a 543-bp coding sequence.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: AnDHN is proposed as a promising candidate gene for engineering crops with enhanced tolerance to multiple abiotic stresses. Overexpression in Arabidopsis confers improved drought, salt, and cold tolerance via deeper root systems, better water retention, osmotic adjustment, and ROS scavenging, suggesting that introducing AnDHN or its orthologs into legume and other crop species could help sustain yield and quality under increasing environmental stresses.
